title: chenopodium hircinum subf. crataeginum
intro: Chenopodium hircinum subf. crataeginum, also known as desert lamb's quarters, is a plant species within the genus Chenopodium, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Chenopodium hircinum subf. crataeginum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
